title: Step 1: Trace downward from the foot location to find the geometry. If the surface is walkable, save the Impact Location and Normal.
comment: Step 1: Trace downward from the foot location to find the geometry. If the surface is walkable, save the Impact Location and Normal.
terms: Step 1: Trace downward from the foot location to find the geometry. If the surface is walkable, save the Impact Location and Normal.

title: Step 1.1: Find the difference in location from the Impact point and the expected (flat) floor location. These values are offset by the nomrmal multiplied by the foot height to get better behavior on angled surfaces.
comment: Step 1.1: Find the difference in location from the Impact point and the expected (flat) floor location. These values are offset by the nomrmal multiplied by the foot height to get better behavior on angled surfaces.

title: Step 2: Interp the Current Location Offset to the new target value. Interpolate at different speeds based on whether the new target is above or below the current one.
comment: Step 2: Interp the Current Location Offset to the new target value. Interpolate at different speeds based on whether the new target is above or below the current one.

title: Step 3: Interp the Current Rotation Offset to the new target value.
comment: Step 3: Interp the Current Rotation Offset to the new target value.
terms: Step 3: Interp the Current Rotation Offset to the new target value.

title: Only update Foot IK offset values if the Foot IK curve has a weight. If it equals 0, clear the offset values.
comment: Only update Foot IK offset values if the Foot IK curve has a weight. If it equals 0, clear the offset values.
terms: Only update Foot IK offset values if the Foot IK curve has a weight. If it equals 0, clear the offset values.

title: Step 1.2: Calculate the Rotation offset by getting the Atan2 of the Impact Normal.
comment: Step 1.2: Calculate the Rotation offset by getting the Atan2 of the Impact Normal.
terms: Step 1.2: Calculate the Rotation offset by getting the Atan2 of the Impact Normal.

title: Calculate the Pelvis Alpha by finding the average Foot IK weight. If the alpha is 0, clear the offset.
comment: Calculate the Pelvis Alpha by finding the average Foot IK weight. If the alpha is 0, clear the offset.
terms: Calculate the Pelvis Alpha by finding the average Foot IK weight. If the alpha is 0, clear the offset.

title: Step 1: Set the new Pelvis Target to be the lowest Foot Offset
comment: Step 1: Set the new Pelvis Target to be the lowest Foot Offset
terms: Step 1: Set the new Pelvis Target to be the lowest Foot Offset

title: Step 2: Interp the Current Pelvis Offset to the new target value. Interpolate at different speeds based on whether the new target is above or below the current one.
comment: Step 2: Interp the Current Pelvis Offset to the new target value. Interpolate at different speeds based on whether the new target is above or below the current one.

title: Enable Movement Animations if IsMoving and HasMovementInput, or if the Speed is greater than 150.
comment: Enable Movement Animations if IsMoving and HasMovementInput, or if the Speed is greater than 150.
terms: Enable Movement Animations if IsMoving and HasMovementInput, or if the Speed is greater than 150.

title:
Turn in Place
La destinazione è ALS Anim BP
terms:
Turn in Place
La destinazione è ALS Anim BP

title: Step 1: Check if Aiming angle is outside of the Turn Check Min Angle, and if the Aim Yaw Rate is below the Aim Yaw Rate Limit. If so, begin counting the Elapsed Delay Time. If not, reset the Elapsed Delay Time. This ensures the conditions remain true for a sustained peroid of time before turning in place.
comment: Step 1: Check if Aiming angle is outside of the Turn Check Min Angle, and if the Aim Yaw Rate is below the Aim Yaw Rate Limit. If so, begin counting the Elapsed Delay Time. If not, reset the Elapsed Delay Time. This ensures the conditions remain true for a sustained peroid of time before turning in place.

title: Step 2: Check if the Elapsed Delay time exceeds the set delay (mapped to the turn angle range). If so, trigger a Turn In Place.
comment: Step 2: Check if the Elapsed Delay time exceeds the set delay (mapped to the turn angle range). If so, trigger a Turn In Place.
terms: Step 2: Check if the Elapsed Delay time exceeds the set delay (mapped to the turn angle range). If so, trigger a Turn In Place.
